title: Labels should be avoided
description:
Labels should not be usedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingMore InfoIn JavaScript, labels are identifiers that allow you to name blocks of code, such as loops and conditional statements.  They are used in conjunction
with statements likebreakandcontinueto control the flow of execution within nested loops and conditionals. It’s worth noting that labels are not widely used in modern JavaScript programming because they can lead to complex and hard-to-maintain code.  In
most cases, there are better alternatives to achieve the desired control flow without resorting to labels.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Labels should be avoided as they make code harder to maintain and understand
Labeled break/continue statements should be avoided - consider refactoring with functions or flags
